creator: mark@kyne.com.au
text:
Hi Ryo,

On Wed, Jun 13, 2001 at 01:02:28PM +0900, Ryo MIYAMOTO wrote:
> Is it possible that
>   * checksum error will be ignored by NO_CHECKSUM=yes in Makefile, or
>   * messages to users say 
>       ``Use NO_CHECKSUM=yes, if only at the checksum error.'' 

The checksum is necessary to ensure against trojans and the like. This
doesn't happen very often - but it has happened. If the Molden port
bypasses the checksum, or recommends users bypass it manually then
there is no protection whatsoever.

That leaves 3 options:
1) Convince the authors to start using patch levels.
2) Don't use the most recent version, ie use 3.6 so it doesn't change
   silently again.
3) Use the latest version and be ready to update whenever the authors
   repackage.

I think the options are preferable in that order. Using option 3 will
only cause a lot more work for everyone involved and may leave the
port broken come release time. As I see it the only valid reason to
use the latest version is if it contains "must have" improvements over
3.6.

You could try convincing the authors from your point of view as the
FreeBSD maintainer. If that doesn't work, do you have any objections
to leaving the port at version 3.6 to prevent future checksum
breakage?

Regards,
Mark
